title: What Good QA Actually Owns
description: A practical breakdown of what strong QA ownership looks like beyond clicking through tickets and counting defects.
promise: Good QA owns confidence, risk, feedback, and the shape of the release conversation.

title: Regression Pass Rate Is Not Release Confidence
description: Why pass rate is useful but dangerous when treated as the main signal for release readiness.
promise: A pass rate is a signal, not a verdict.

title: My Practical Release Readiness Checklist
description: A field-ready checklist for deciding whether a release is actually understood, not merely finished.
promise: A release checklist should expose risk, not create ceremony.

title: How I Review A Failed Nightly Automation Run
description: A repeatable triage pattern for making failed automation runs useful instead of noisy.
promise: A failed nightly run is only useful when it becomes a decision record.

title: When Not To Automate A Test
description: A practical filter for deciding whether automation will increase confidence or just add maintenance drag.
promise: Automation should buy confidence faster than it spends maintenance.

title: Test Data Is Usually The Real E2E Problem
description: Why E2E testing often fails because of data control, not because the test idea is wrong.
promise: Most E2E pain is data pain wearing a different mask.

title: What Vox Mana Taught Me About Data Contracts
description: How a side project reinforced the value of source layers, generated data boundaries, and explicit contracts.
promise: Side projects become useful when they force you to name the system boundaries.

title: How I Use AI/Codex Without Losing Control Of The Project
description: A practical workflow for using AI assistants while keeping ownership, scope, and decision history clear.
promise: AI is useful when the workflow keeps the human in control of scope and judgment.
